summary: The Technology Radar is Thoughtworks' biannual publication that categorizes emerging technologies into four recommendation levels: Adopt, Trial, Assess, and Hold. Volume 33 (November 2025) focuses heavily on AI infrastructure and agentic workflows, highlighting how GPU orchestration has become essential for AI workloads and how the Model Context Protocol (MCP) is transforming how agents operate. The Radar also examines AI coding workflows and their impact on software development, while warning about emerging AI antipatterns that teams should avoid. This edition reflects the industry's rapid evolution around AI tools and practices, emphasizing that human judgment remains critical even as AI accelerates engineering work.
key_points: GPU-aware orchestration has become table stakes for AI workloads, with topology now a first-class scheduling concern for multi-GPU training and inference
MCP (Model Context Protocol) has become the ultimate integration protocol for powering agents and enabling semi-autonomous agentic workflows
AI coding workflows are embedding across the entire software value chain, from understanding legacy codebases to forward engineering
Emerging AI antipatterns require vigilance, including AI-accelerated shadow IT, naive API-to-MCP conversion, and complacency with AI-generated code
AI must amplify entire teams rather than just individual contributors, driving practices like curated shared instructions
money_quotes: GPU‑aware orchestration is becoming table stakes — topology is now a first‑class scheduling concern.
In many ways, MCP has become the ultimate integration protocol for powering agents and enabling them to work efficiently and semi-autonomously.
While AI can accelerate engineering, human judgment is still indispensable.
AI must amplify the entire team, not just individual contributors.
Teams should stay vigilant for patterns that appear effective at first but degrade over time and slow feedback, undermine adaptability or obscure accountability.
Complacency with AI-generated code remains a shared concern.
key_insight: As AI reshapes software development infrastructure and workflows, organizations must balance rapid adoption with vigilance against emerging antipatterns while ensuring AI amplifies entire teams rather than just individuals.
